q:
watch TV
 (earlier: watching TV / now: TV off)
 She ___.
a: She has been watching TV.

q:
play tennis
 (earlier: playing tennis / now: game finished)
 They ___.
a: They have been playing tennis.

q:
swim
 (You meet Kate leaving the pool)
 Hi, Kate. ___ (you / swim)?
a: Have you been swimming?

q:
wait
 (You arrive late to meet Ben)
 I'm sorry I'm late, Ben. ___ (you / wait / long)?
a: Have you been waiting long?

q:
do
 (Jane's boy has a dirty face and hands)
 Why are you so dirty? ___ (what / you / do)?
a: What have you been doing?

q:
work
 (You see Anna in a shop unexpectedly)
 Hi, Anna. ___ (how long / you / work / here)?
a: How long have you been working here?

q:
do
 (A friend tells you he sells phones)
 You sell phones? ___ (how long / you / do / that)?
a: How long have you been doing that?

q:
rain
 It's raining. The rain started two hours ago.
 It ___ for two hours.
a: It's been raining for two hours.

q:
wait
 We are waiting for the bus. We started waiting 20 minutes ago.
 We ___ for 20 minutes.
a: We have been waiting for 20 minutes.

q:
learn
 I'm learning Japanese. I started classes in December.
 I ___ since December.
a: I've been learning Japanese since December.

q:
work
 Jessica is working in a hotel. She started working there on 18 January.
 Jessica ___ since 18 January.
a: Jessica has been working in a hotel since 18 January.

q:
go
 Our friends always go to Italy for their holidays. The first time was years ago.
 Our friends ___ to Italy for years.
a: Our friends have been going to Italy for years.

q:
learn
 Maria ___ (Maria / learn) English for two years.
a: Maria has been learning English for two years.

q:
look
 Hi, Tom. ___ (I / look) for you. I need to ask you something.
a: Hi, Tom. I've been looking for you. I need to ask you something.

q:
look
 Why ___ (you / look) at me like that? Stop it!
a: Why are you looking at me like that? Stop it!

q:
teach
 Rachel is a teacher. ___ (she / teach) for ten years.
a: Rachel is a teacher. She has been teaching for ten years.

q:
think
 ___ (I / think) about what you said and I've decided to take your advice.
a: I've been thinking about what you said and I've decided to take your advice.

q:
work
 'Is Paul on holiday this week?' 'No, ___ (he / work).'
a: 'Is Paul on holiday this week?' 'No, he's working.'

q:
work
 Sarah is very tired. ___ (she / work) very hard recently.
a: Sarah is very tired. She has been working very hard recently.

q:
drive
 It's dangerous to use your phone when ___ (you / drive).
a: It's dangerous to use your phone when you're driving.

q:
travel
 Laura ___ (travel) in South America for the last three months.
a: Laura has been traveling in South America for the last three months.
